crime: One supper the victim read the round of small debts aloud — who owed whom a dessert, a shift, a shower turn — and every girl was in it somewhere. Her name was nowhere. The victim said, to laughter, that she was not part of the economy.
The victim swam the lake each midday, her phone propped on the bank to stream it; the girls watched from the lounge. That noon she had carried her mending to the trees south of the bank.
Mid-swim the stream showed the swimmer fold and go straight down. For two heartbeats a thin taut line broke the surface, running shoreward toward the trees; then the water was smooth.
Her flat braid-cord — the mending cord the girls had seen at her belt a hundred times — had looped the swimmer's ankles and drawn her under. Then it came back to her hand and left the lake empty.
The drowned girl had thin even welts circling both ankles, patterned like a flat braid. The girls dragged the lake and brought up nothing that could have made them.
From that day the braid-cord was gone from her belt. She came to the dragging with a plain rope; the girls had seen the braid daily and they marked the absence.
The welts matched the cord and the cord vanished the same day — her cord was the weapon. A weapon that left the lake on its own means her magic.
She told the court she spent midday in the courtyard. The stream's opening frame shows a figure in the tree-shade south of the bank, seated with mending across the knees.
fields.epitaph: Nothing of hers ever rested in another girl's hands, and so nothing of her did either.
execution: A great loom stood on the execution stand. She was bound upright inside the frame, against the backing cloth.
Shuttles drove thread across the frame, layer above layer, working upward from her feet.
The weave took the legs, the arms, the shoulders — each course binding her tighter to the backing.
The last courses closed over her nose and mouth, and the frame filled with cloth.

magic.name: At Her Bidding
magic.text: Her own belongings move at her will wherever she is, and answer no hand but hers.
